title: GTC Taipei could change everything we think about AI stocks
author: Imaginary-Library-80
selftext:
Whenever Jensen Huang steps on stage, the AI market tends to react like it’s hearing a new set of rules being written in real time. That’s why NVIDIA GTC Taipei is being watched so closely, not just as another keynote, but as a potential reset point for how the entire AI trade is priced and positioned.  
  
The interesting part this time is how the narrative has shifted. It’s no longer just about GPUs or raw chip demand. The focus is expanding into AI PCs, enterprise deployment, robotics and full-stack infrastructure. In other words, AI is moving from “build phase” into “scale everywhere phase,” and that usually means the winners don’t stay concentrated in one corner of the market.  
  
That’s also why I have stopped looking at AI as a single stock story. My watchlist has been broader lately,  NVIDIA, Microsoft, AMD, Arm Holdings, Broadcom, TSMC, Dell Technologies, Micron Technology, Super Micro Computer, and Intel. Each one sits in a different part of the AI stack, so the rotation depends heavily on what narrative the keynote strengthens.  
  
To stay ahead of that rotation, I have also been using GetAgent as a way to filter where momentum could shift next across the AI ecosystem. Instead of chasing headlines, it helps narrow down where capital might actually flow when narratives expand.  
  
If GTC Taipei reinforces demand across AI infrastructure, enterprise adoption, and next-gen computing, then this doesn’t look like the end of the AI cycle, it looks more like another expansion phase. And in cycles like this, the biggest moves often come from where attention rotates next, not where it already is.
